name: Louis XVI
description:
(1754 A.D. -1793 A.D.) Louis XVI was the last king of France before the Bourbon monarchy fell during the French Revolution. He became king in 1774 following the death of his grandfather, Louis XV. His reign was marked by attempts at reform, including efforts to improve religious tolerance and economic policies, but he faced strong opposition from the nobility.

Louis XVI also supported the American colonies in their fight for independence from Britain, which contributed to France's financial crisis. As tensions in France escalated, he was forced to recognize the authority of the National Assembly after the storming of the Bastille. Eventually, he was arrested and found guilty of treason. He was executed by guillotine on January 21, 1793. His wife, Marie Antoinette, was executed later that year.
